avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. The sometimes deep wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant on steep shady slopes above approximately 2600 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The avalanches can as before reach medium size.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: The new snow of the weekend is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in some places.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1700 m.
The easterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow. The various wind slabs have bonded generally well with the old snowpack. The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases in particular on very steep shady slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable to the trained eye.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of wet avalanches will decrease.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Wednesday above approximately 1700 m. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.
